# v43 — Tranche 6: Declarative `<Flow>` API + AI + Docs

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Deliver the **easy-to-use, AI-first** headline surface and the auto-generated documentation. Ship a
single declarative **`<Flow nodes={…} edges={…} />`** that takes **plain serializable data** an agent can emit
as JSON (no imperative graph building), with optional `nodeTypes`/`edgeTypes` maps for custom renderers and
`onConnect`/`onNodesChange`/`onEdgesChange` callbacks. Ship an optional **dependency-free `gridLayout`/
`layeredLayout`** helper for the "just arrange these for me" case. Land per-primitive **manifests** (incl.
`intent` **and `examples`**), **auto-generated storybook stories** under `apps/storybook/stories/flow/`,
**auto-generated docs**, and a **`scaffold_flow`** MCP/skill affordance so agents generate a flow from intent.

**Architecture:** `<Flow>` composes the T2–T5 primitives: it owns a `useFlow` store seeded from `nodes`/`edges`
props (controllable), renders `<FlowCanvas>` + `<FlowBackground>` + a `<FlowNode>` per node (resolving
`nodeTypes[type]` or a default renderer) + a `<FlowEdge>` per edge (resolving `edgeTypes[type]`) + optional
`<FlowControls>`/`<FlowMiniMap>`, and wires `useConnection`. `layout.ts` provides pure functions that compute
`position`s for nodes lacking them. Per CLAUDE.md: `useSignals()` first; no banned hooks; i18n-defaulted
strings; serializable props (the AI-first contract).

**Stories are auto-generated, not hand-authored.** Today storybook stories are hand-written files governed by
`scripts/quality/story-check.ts` (`pnpm audit:stories` — every `*.stories.tsx` must map to a registry entry).
This tranche realises CLAUDE.md's stated goal ("Storybook — auto-generated stories from manifests") for flow by
adding a **`stories:generate`** step that reads each flow `*.meta.ts`'s `examples` (`{ title, code,
description }`) and **emits** `apps/storybook/stories/flow/<name>.stories.tsx`, wired into `pnpm regen` so the
stories regenerate (and the drift gate catches staleness). The generator is generic — when the `flow-story`
storyline meta lands in **T7**, its example becomes a generated story with no extra work. `audit:stories` stays
green because every generated story maps to a `flow/*` registry entry.

**Tech Stack:** signal-driven TSX + CSS Modules; the T1–T5 `@cascivo/flow` primitives; owned layout math;
`@cascivo/core`; `@cascivo/i18n`; a Node `stories:generate` script (mirrors the existing `scripts/*/generate.ts`
codegen idiom) feeding Storybook; the registry/MCP/docs pipeline (`pnpm regen`); Vitest + Testing Library.

---

## Files

- Create: `packages/flow/src/flows/flow/flow.tsx`, `.module.css`, `.meta.ts`, `.test.tsx`
- Create: `packages/flow/src/engine/layout.ts` + `layout.test.ts`
- Create: `scripts/stories/generate.ts` (emits `apps/storybook/stories/flow/*.stories.tsx` from flow metas' `examples`)
- Create (generated): `apps/storybook/stories/flow/*.stories.tsx` (one per flow primitive + the declarative `<Flow>` example)
- Modify: `package.json` (add `stories:generate`; chain it into `regen`)
- Modify: `packages/flow/src/index.ts` (export `Flow` + layout helpers)
- Modify: `packages/mcp/src/*` (a `scaffold_flow` affordance) and/or `skills/` (a flow section)
- Modify: `packages/flow/README.md` / `readme.body.md` (usage); docs auto-generate from manifests

---

### Task 1: Study the declarative pattern, the chart stories, and the MCP/skill surface

**Files:** `packages/charts/src/charts/*/*.tsx` (a data-driven chart), `apps/storybook/stories/chart/*.stories.tsx`, `packages/mcp/src/server.ts`, `skills/`

- [ ] **Step 1:** Read a data-driven chart component + its story to mirror the declarative-data + story idiom.
- [ ] **Step 2:** Read `packages/mcp/src/server.ts` (how tools/affordances are registered) to plan a
      `scaffold_flow` affordance that returns a starter `<Flow nodes edges />` from an intent description.
- [ ] **Step 3:** Read a `skills/` entry to plan a short flow section (when to reach for `@cascivo/flow`, the
      declarative shape, the basic-set scope).

### Task 2: `<Flow>` — the declarative API

**Files:** `packages/flow/src/flows/flow/flow.tsx`, `.module.css`

- [ ] **Step 1:** Props: `nodes: FlowNode[]`, `edges: FlowEdge[]` (controllable via `useControllableSignal`),
      `nodeTypes?`/`edgeTypes?` (maps `type` → renderer), `onConnect?`, `onNodesChange?`, `onEdgesChange?`,
      `fitView?` (default true), `background?`, `controls?`, `minimap?`, `layout?` (`'grid'|'layered'|fn`),
      `className`/`...rest`. `useSignals()` first.
- [ ] **Step 2:** Seed `useFlow` from props; if `layout` is set, compute missing positions via `layout.ts`.
      Render `<FlowCanvas>` + (optional) `<FlowBackground>`/`<FlowControls>`/`<FlowMiniMap>`, a `<FlowNode>`
      per node (default renderer shows `data.label`; custom via `nodeTypes`), a `<FlowEdge>` per edge; wire
      `useConnection` → `onConnect`. Emit `onNodesChange`/`onEdgesChange` on drag/connect.
- [ ] **Step 3:** `flow.module.css`: thin wrapper; tokenised; sensible default `min-block-size`; no literals.

### Task 3: `engine/layout.ts` — optional dependency-free layout

**Files:** `packages/flow/src/engine/layout.ts`

- [ ] **Step 1:** `gridLayout(nodes, { columns?, gap? })` → nodes with computed `position`s in a grid. Pure.
- [ ] **Step 2:** `layeredLayout(nodes, edges, { direction?, gap? })` → a simple longest-path layering for
      DAGs (no Dagre/ELK; a minimal topological pass). Pure, deterministic, documented as "basic — bring your
      own layout for complex graphs."

### Task 4: Manifests + the story generator (auto-generated stories)

**Files:** `packages/flow/src/flows/flow/flow.meta.ts`, every flow `*.meta.ts` (ensure rich `examples`), `scripts/stories/generate.ts`, `package.json`, `apps/storybook/stories/flow/*.stories.tsx` (generated output)

- [ ] **Step 1:** `flow.meta.ts`: full `ComponentMeta` (`category: 'display'`, props incl. `nodes`/`edges`/
      `nodeTypes`/`layout`, tokens, a11y, `examples` — a minimal declarative flow, custom node types, animated
      edges, layered layout — `dependencies: ['@cascivo/core']`, tags, full `intent`). Confirm **every** flow
      primitive's meta carries both `intent` (so `intent-completeness.test.ts` passes) and a non-empty
      `examples` array (the story generator's input).
- [ ] **Step 2:** Write `scripts/stories/generate.ts` (mirror the `scripts/registry/generate.ts` codegen idiom):
      read each flow `*.meta.ts`, and for each emit `apps/storybook/stories/flow/<name>.stories.tsx` — a
      `Meta`/`StoryObj` file titled `Flow/<Name>` with one named export per `meta.examples[]` entry (the
      `code`/`title`/`description`), wrapped in the standard sizing decorator + theme switch. Mark each file with
      an `AUTO-GENERATED — do not edit; run pnpm stories:generate` header.
- [ ] **Step 3:** Add `"stories:generate": "node scripts/stories/generate.ts"` to `package.json` and chain it
      into `regen` (after `registry:generate`, before `audit`). Run it; the `apps/storybook/stories/flow/`
      folder is produced. Confirm `pnpm audit:stories` is green (each generated story maps to a `flow/*`
      registry entry) and that re-running leaves no drift.

### Task 5: AI affordance + README

**Files:** `packages/mcp/src/*`, `skills/*`, `packages/flow/README.md`, `readme.body.md`

- [ ] **Step 1:** Add a `scaffold_flow` MCP affordance (returns a starter declarative `<Flow nodes edges />`
      from an intent description) and a `skills/` flow section (when to reach for `@cascivo/flow`, the
      declarative shape, the basic-set scope).
- [ ] **Step 2:** Fill `README.md`/`readme.body.md` with the declarative quick-start; docs pages auto-generate
      from the manifests via the existing pipeline.

### Task 6: Tests, regen, exports

**Files:** `packages/flow/src/flows/flow/flow.test.tsx`, `packages/flow/src/engine/layout.test.ts`, `packages/flow/src/index.ts`

- [ ] **Step 1:** `flow.test.tsx`: renders a node per `nodes` + an edge per `edges`; `nodeTypes` custom
      renderer used; `onConnect` fires from a simulated handle drag; `layout='grid'` assigns positions.
- [ ] **Step 2:** `layout.test.ts`: `gridLayout` places N nodes deterministically; `layeredLayout` layers a
      known DAG correctly.
- [ ] **Step 3:** Static check: no banned hooks. Export `Flow` + `gridLayout`/`layeredLayout` from
      `src/index.ts`. `pnpm regen` (registry gains `flow/*` entries; `stories:generate` emits the flow stories;
      docs pick them up); drift gate clean. `pnpm exec vp run @cascivo/flow#test` green; `pnpm audit:stories`
      green; storybook builds.

### Task 7: Commit T6

- [ ] **Step 1: Stage and commit**

  ```bash
  git add packages/flow/src/flows/flow packages/flow/src/engine/layout.ts packages/flow/src/engine/layout.test.ts packages/flow/src/index.ts scripts/stories/generate.ts package.json apps/storybook/stories/flow packages/mcp/src skills packages/flow/README.md packages/flow/readme.body.md registry.json
  git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
  feat(flow): declarative <Flow> API, layout, auto-gen stories + AI (v43 T6)

  The easy-to-use, AI-first surface: a single declarative <Flow nodes edges/>
  taking plain serializable data (agent-emittable JSON) with nodeTypes/
  edgeTypes maps, onConnect/onNodesChange/onEdgesChange, and optional
  dependency-free gridLayout/layeredLayout. Adds scripts/stories/generate.ts
  (chained into pnpm regen) that emits apps/storybook/stories/flow/* from each
  flow meta's examples — stories are auto-generated, not hand-authored.
  Per-primitive manifests (with intent + examples), auto-generated docs, and a
  scaffold_flow MCP/skill affordance. No banned hooks; i18n-defaulted.

  Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
  EOF
  )"
  ```
